title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple explains the motive behind Heather Badcock’s death to Jason Radcliffe and Detective Constable Caddle. She reveals that years prior, Heather had attended a celebration while suffering from measles to obtain an autograph from the movie star Marina Gregg. During this encounter, Heather unknowingly infected the pregnant Marina with measles, which resulted in Marina's child being born with severe physical and mental defects.

Miss Marple asserts that when Heather met Marina again and boasted about her bravery in attending the event while ill, Marina acted impulsively. Marina had already placed camphor, a dangerous drug, into her own drink. When Heather's cocktail spilled, Marina manipulated the situation by insisting that Heather drink from Marina's own cup to clean up the mess. To deflect suspicion, Marina had staged threatening letters and self-poisoning incidents to make herself appear as the intended victim. Miss Marple suggests that Jason Radcliffe had known the truth about the measles all along and had been attempting to protect Marina from legal consequences or further harm.

After Detective Inspector Tidler interrupts the conversation, Miss Marple visits Marina Gregg, only to find her lying dead on a bed. Miss Marple muses whether Marina died from a self-administered overdose of pills or if someone else provided them. Meanwhile, Jason Radcliffe reflects on Marina's beauty and her history of suffering.
